inline_comments.message: I think this would be better as just getDataspaces

We try to avoid the acronyms in cps, also this can be done easier in groovy by doing the following:
dataspaces = [ dataspaceName ]

or remove lines 234 & 235 altogether and do this on 236
mockCpsAdminService.getAllDataspaces() >> [ dataspaceName ]
I don't think we need this new object, this method can just return a string
Would prefer not to use acronym ds
if we could use the java 8 .stream().map() we can save making another list
I think we also need to test the error scenario: unknown dataspace
since we are only testing 1 scenario, we can remove the highlighted. same with below

NB: typo thrown
(NB just means non blocking, its for situations in which we see something that we would like changed but are ok with it being merged)
NB: i think this is better reversed (given: 'an endpoint' ... and 'service method returns...' -> same as the method below
I would suggest like getAllDataspacesForGivenDName... but it is a bit long... what do the team think?

I am sure we have something that could be used?
and do we need a full bean just to hold a string?
it is as per other GET operation test cases like 'get anchor'
suggestion , may add one more 'someDataspace1' 'someDataspace2' ? I think this test is similar to test above, maybe adding one more shows it returns multiple?

NB, this is a bit ambiguous. I would  have mentioned the speci objects instead of suggesting we support some wildcard option :-) But is is only a commit message (still people will see this and wonder)

dataspace name
add: given an...  
(see previous existing methods and try to be consistent)

Unlike other responses, we are NOT modelling it in the API ie. we don't have object in  org.onap.cps.rest.model.
I think this is a more loose contract then other API methods, inconsistent and should be addressed

Maybe since these are only names, its OK but I would like to discuss

Unlike other responses, we are NOT modelling it in the API ie. we don't have object in  org.onap.cps.rest.model.
I think this is a more loose contract then other API methods, inconsistent and should be addressed
NB: Order of test inconsistent with order in API and Impl
always test collection with 2 value (No more, no less unless for very good (test)reasons)
makes me wonder; what is the point of this (API) method, just proofing it exists?!
attempting to retrieve a non-existing dataspace
not so much invalid (would suggest an invalid name) but rather non-existing

again what is the point?!
If it is just to prof it exists maybe we should return a boolean?!
now we are returning Dataspace object.
Change to v1 same for below
NB can use [ ] instead of Set.of()
Using var will cause issues in sonar later on so we don't use var at all
I don't see the need for this class
since objectUnderTest.getAllDataspaces() returning Collection, we can't use [].
This model was added later on request of Toine to have consistency with other APIs
Was discussed already, it's following what we have done for other database objects
Should replace with @Getter and @Setter to avoid additional unnecessary methods
getAllDataspaces() is returning collection object which can't be casted to []
In Groovy [ ] equates to an arraylist and then [ ] as set creates a set, fine here but for future knowledge
